focus: rename artifact existence, stale filename references, and implementation-summary claims

findingDetails.title: Gemini improve command still points at obsolete YAML asset names
findingDetails.evidence: Gemini TOML and README reference deep_start-agent-improvement-loop YAML filenames while OpenCode/Claude references use deep_start-agent-improvement-loop and renamed asset directories contain only the new names.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update Gemini command TOML and README to renamed YAML asset names or document that Gemini does not load YAML assets.
findingDetails.scopeProof: OpenCode/Claude command docs use renamed assets; Gemini TOML/README still use old assets; Gemini improve directory has no assets directory with old-name files.

findingDetails.title: Active manual-testing playbook commands still load the removed agent path
findingDetails.evidence: CP-041 and CP-042 executable command blocks still cat .opencode/agents/improve-agent.md after the active agent file was renamed.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update active playbook command blocks and evidence table wording to .opencode/agents/deep-agent-improvement.md.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Runtime agent glob returned only deep-agent-improvement files; CP-041/CP-042 active playbooks still use old path.

findingDetails.title: Completion artifact still contains required-population placeholders while claiming COMPLETE
findingDetails.evidence: Implementation summary claims COMPLETE and REQ-010 met while retaining [POPULATE] placeholders in metadata, narrative, commit evidence, limitations, and cross-reference sections.
findingDetails.recommendation: Populate or remove placeholders before claiming implementation-summary completion.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Contradiction is local to implementation-summary.md complete and populated claims versus unresolved placeholder lines.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Agent renames present; Gemini command asset references stale.
Implementation-summary completion evidence contradicted by placeholders.
Active playbook commands retain old agent path.
Four renamed runtime agent files exist and old active agent paths were absent in glob.

noveltyJustification: First iteration found three new correctness P1s across Gemini command routing, active playbook executable paths, and completion artifact evidence; no prior iteration findings existed.
edgeCases: Delta file requested by dispatch and written under review packet root.
Historical z_archive/spec references treated out of scope unless active files claim current behavior.
Gemini has no local assets directory in checked listing, so stale YAML references may be command-text drift rather than missing local files.

focus: traceability alignment across packet ledgers, resource-map classifications, requirement evidence, active references, and spec mirrors

findingDetails.title: Completion claims are not traceable to the task/checklist ledgers
findingDetails.evidence: Implementation summary claims COMPLETE and all P0/P1 acceptance criteria met, but tasks completion criteria remain unchecked at tasks.md:128-131 and checklist totals remain [ ]/17 P0 and [ ]/12 P1 at checklist.md:139-140.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either update task/checklist ledgers with evidence after verification, or downgrade implementation-summary completion claims until the ledgers are complete.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Contradiction is packet-local across implementation-summary.md:33, tasks.md:128-131, and checklist.md:139-140; mirror docs carry the same unchecked state.

findingDetails.title: Resource map marks active stale-reference surfaces OK
findingDetails.evidence: Resource-map OK rows cover Gemini command docs and active CP-041/CP-042 playbooks, but active evidence still shows obsolete Gemini YAML names and old .opencode/agents/improve-agent.md cat commands.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update the resource map to mark these rows failing/needs-fix until F001/F002 are remediated, and use active line evidence rather than planned target state.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Resource-map OK rows at lines 57, 83, and 112-113 conflict with active stale evidence at .gemini/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.toml:158, .gemini/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.toml:60, CP-041:77, and CP-042:73.

unchanged /deep:start-agent-improvement-loop and Gemini command filename were not treated as defects

focus: maintainability of stale-reference concentration, runtime doc drift, and repair guidance after rename

findingDetails.title: Active skill catalog and benchmark defaults still publish the removed proposal-agent/YAML identity
findingDetails.evidence: Active feature-catalog docs still delegate candidate generation to `.opencode/agents/improve-agent.md`, list obsolete `deep_start-agent-improvement-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml` workflow assets, and the default benchmark profile targets `.opencode/agents/improve-agent.md` even though the active agent inventory contains `deep-agent-improvement.md` instead.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update active feature-catalog pages, SKILL integration points, and benchmark profile defaults to the `deep-agent-improvement` agent/YAML names; rerun the old-name inventory outside `specs/` and `z_archive/` before marking maintainability complete.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Old proposal-agent/YAML references appear in feature catalog, SKILL integration docs, and default benchmark config beyond prior F001/F002 surfaces; `.opencode/agents/` inventory contains no `improve-agent.md`.

traceabilityChecks: Remaining stale references are broader than F001/F002 and require a same-class active-scope inventory pass.
OpenCode and Claude command docs use renamed YAML assets; Gemini command docs remain prior F001 drift.
Follow-on repair path is concrete but current packet ledgers remain misleading due to F003-F005.
Exact active old YAML/agent-path references were checked outside historical z_archive/spec archives.
edgeCases: Historical specs and z_archive references excluded unless active files rely on them as current evidence.
.claude/settings.local.json old move commands treated as local settings artifact, not active command/runtime docs in scope.
F006 excludes already-recorded Gemini and CP-041/CP-042 stale surfaces.
ruledOut: No duplicate F001 for Gemini YAML references.
No duplicate F002 for CP-041/CP-042 old cat commands.
No new P0 because evidence shows maintainability drift, not immediate exploitability or destructive data loss.
